prompt: A master tailor's workshop, warmly lit with overhead studio lighting. At the center of the room stands a tailor's dress mannequin wearing a perfectly fitted dark navy bespoke suit — every seam, dart, and fold obsessively calibrated to the exact contours of that specific mannequin. The suit on the mannequin looks immaculate: sharp lapels, perfect shoulder line, every button lying flat. Surrounding the mannequin in a loose semicircle, five real people of visibly different body types each wearing an identical copy of the same suit. On every single person the suit fails completely and differently. The tailor stands proudly to one side, pointing at the mannequin with satisfaction. No text, no labels, no measuring tape with numbers visible, no diagrams, no equations. Photorealistic, cinematic documentary photography, shallow depth of field focused on the mannequin, warm golden workshop light. Negative prompt: no text, no labels, no floating elements, no annotations, no diagrams, no neural networks, no glowing elements, no abstract data flows, no geometric overlays, no equations, no mathematical notation
metaphor: A suit tailored obsessively to one mannequin fits it perfectly but fails completely on every real person — mirroring how an overfit model memorizes training data but fails to generalize.
